messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\n\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS\nPrevious validation issues:\nNone",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "initialize-project",
      "title": "Initialize Node + Express project",
      "description": "Create a new Node project, install Express and developer tools, add scripts (start, dev), and initialize a Git repo.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "package.json created, express installed, npm scripts for start/dev present, repository initialized."
    },
    {
      "id": "setup-tailwind",
      "title": "Install and configure Tailwind CSS",
      "description": "Install Tailwind and PostCSS, create tailwind.config.js and input CSS file, configure build/postcss scripts and ensure static CSS output is generated.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "initialize-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind config and CSS file exist, npm build generates a final CSS file that can be served to the client."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Create Express server and static file serving",
      "description": "Implement an Express server that serves static assets, serves the main HTML page at root (/), and is configured to parse JSON bodies for API endpoints.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "initialize-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Server starts without errors, root route serves HTML, static folder accessible, JSON body parsing works."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-color-api",
      "title": "Implement color GET/POST API with simple persistence",
      "description": "Add REST endpoints to get and set the 'hello world' color. Store the color in a simple in-memory variable with optional JSON file persistence so color survives restarts.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "GET /api/color returns current color, POST /api/color updates color and responds with new value, color persists to a JSON file if implemented."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Create frontend page and color-picker UI using Tailwind",
      "description": "Build the HTML page that displays the 'hello world' text styled with Tailwind and add a color input (or palette) UI to choose colors; include the compiled Tailwind CSS.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-tailwind"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Page loads with Tailwind styles, displays 'hello world', includes a working color input element in the UI."
    },
    {
      "id": "integrate-and-test",
      "title": "Connect frontend to API and perform manual tests",
      "description": "Wire the color input to call the API endpoints (fetch GET/POST), update the 'hello world' text color live on success, and run a set of manual tests to verify behavior and persistence.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-color-api",
        "build-frontend-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Selecting a color updates the text immediately, API calls succeed, color persists after server restart (if persistence implemented), and manual test checklist is completed."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 6,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down incremental: bootstrap project and tooling first, configure styling, implement server and API, build the frontend UI, then integrate and test. Tasks are ordered to avoid blocking dependencies and ensure each step produces testable artifacts."
}
